description: The debate over Federal Reserve Chairman Paul Volcker's high-interest-rate policy to combat stagflation in 1980-1981. Public sentiment shifted from supporting aggressive inflation control to opposing it as unemployment surged and industrial production plummeted.

key_events.description: Volcker announces the 'Saturday Night Special' monetary tightening.
Auto industry layoffs reach crisis levels in Detroit.
Farmers drive tractors to Washington D.C. to protest high rates.
Inflation begins to taper, stabilizing Fed support among elite observers.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how economic hardship overrides long-term strategic support for central bank policies.
